data.meta_description: Book a full moon cruise Marbella 2025 from €749. Sail Puerto Banús at sunset with champagne, tapas & a skipper. Limited availability – reserve now.
data.summary: Experience the magic of a full moon cruise Marbella aboard our premium fleet from Puerto Banús. Includes skipper, drinks, snacks, and stunning coastal views under the moonlight.
key_facts: Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Tier A boats)
Maximum 12 guests per boat (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, etc.)
Includes licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT
Complimentary champagne, cava, beer, soft drinks, and tapas
Perfect for couples, small groups, and celebrations
Available year-round – best on clear summer nights
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations and larger yachts

faq.q: What is included in a full moon cruise Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, a jet ski is also included free for 4-hour bookings.

faq.q: How much does a full moon cruise Marbella cost?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39). An 8-hour charter on these boats is €2,299. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. For other yachts, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boats are available for a full moon cruise?
faq.a: We offer a fleet of 19 boats departing from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Mangusta 80 (12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax), and more. Contact us to match the boat to your group size.

faq.q: What time does a full moon cruise depart?
faq.a: Departures are typically scheduled 30–60 minutes before sunset to catch the golden hour and the moonrise. We can arrange a custom departure time via WhatsApp to suit your plans.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.a: We already provide a selection of drinks and light snacks. If you have specific dietary requirements or want to bring a special cake or champagne, you’re welcome to do so. Please let us know in advance.

faq.q: Is a full moon cruise suitable for children?
faq.a: Yes, children are welcome on board. All our boats have safety equipment and life jackets. We recommend choosing a boat with a spacious deck and cabin, like the Lagoon 380 catamaran, for family comfort.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety comes first. If the forecast shows high winds or rough seas, we will contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

faq.q: How do I book a full moon cruise Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or send a WhatsApp message to our team. We’ll confirm availability, suggest the best boat for your group, and arrange payment. A deposit secures your slot.

data.body_html:
There are few experiences as enchanting as a full moon cruise Marbella. As the sun sinks behind La Concha and the moon rises over the Mediterranean, the coastline transforms into a glittering panorama of lights and shadows. From the sleek superyachts of Puerto Banús to the quiet coves of Cala del Faro, a night on the water under a full moon is pure magic. Whether you're celebrating an anniversary, proposing, or simply escaping the summer crowds, our fleet of 19 boats is ready to take you there.

Why choose a full moon cruise in Marbella?

Marbella's microclimate means even summer evenings are warm, often staying above 25°C until midnight. A full moon cruise lets you dodge the daytime heat while enjoying the best views of the Costa del Sol. You'll sail past the Golden Mile, spot the lights of Estepona in the distance, and maybe anchor near Cabopino for a swim under the stars. The moonlit sea is calm, the air is soft, and the champagne is chilled.

Our fleet for full moon charters

Every boat in our fleet departs from Puerto Banús and comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a full bar. Here are the most popular choices for a moonlit evening:

  • Astondoa 40 – 12.5m, up to 9 guests. From €749 for 2 hours. Intimate and stylish.
  • Azimut 39 – 12.5m, up to 11 guests. Same price tier, more space.
  • Mangusta 80 – 24m, up to 12 guests. Minimum 4 hours at €4,719, includes a free jet ski.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge – 17.6m, 12 guests. Perfect for groups who want a flybridge view of the moon.
  • Lagoon 380 – 11.6m catamaran, 10 guests. Stable and spacious – ideal for families or larger parties.

For smaller groups, the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) offer a more agile, wind-in-your-hair experience. All pricing is transparent – see our boat rental Marbella page for the full list.

What’s included in your full moon cruise

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). Onboard, you'll find unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. For larger bookings on the Mangusta 80, a jet ski is included free for 4-hour charters. You can also bring your own food or decorations – just let us know.

Best routes for a full moon cruise

Your skipper knows the coast intimately. Popular evening routes include:

  • Puerto Banús to Cala del Faro – 20 minutes east, a quiet cove with views of the Marbella skyline.
  • Puerto Banús to Cabopino – 30 minutes west, past the dunes and pine forests.
  • Puerto Banús to Estepona – 45 minutes west, with the moon rising over the Sierra Bermeja.

If you have a specific destination in mind – maybe a dinner stop at a beach club – we can tailor the itinerary. Just ask.

How to book your full moon cruise Marbella

Booking is simple. Check our availability online or send a WhatsApp message. We’ll recommend the best boat for your group size and budget, confirm the date, and take a deposit to secure your slot. For last-minute bookings, call us – we often have a boat ready to go.

Explore all our experiences on the Marbella boat rental hub.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a full moon cruise Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, a jet ski is also included free for 4-hour bookings.

How much does a full moon cruise Marbella cost?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39). An 8-hour charter on these boats is €2,299. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. For other yachts, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

Which boats are available for a full moon cruise?

We offer a fleet of 19 boats departing from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Mangusta 80 (12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax), and more. Contact us to match the boat to your group size.

What time does a full moon cruise depart?

Departures are typically scheduled 30–60 minutes before sunset to catch the golden hour and the moonrise. We can arrange a custom departure time via WhatsApp to suit your plans.

Can I bring my own food or drinks?

We already provide a selection of drinks and light snacks. If you have specific dietary requirements or want to bring a special cake or champagne, you’re welcome to do so. Please let us know in advance.

Is a full moon cruise suitable for children?

Yes, children are welcome on board. All our boats have safety equipment and life jackets. We recommend choosing a boat with a spacious deck and cabin, like the Lagoon 380 catamaran, for family comfort.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety comes first. If the forecast shows high winds or rough seas, we will contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

How do I book a full moon cruise Marbella?

You can book directly through our website or send a WhatsApp message to our team. We’ll confirm availability, suggest the best boat for your group, and arrange payment. A deposit secures your slot.
